text.title: Specification Template: Phase-by-Phase Checklist
text.summary: Ordered checklist for filling each section of spec.md from file creation through quality gate review, covering all 12 phases.

list.item: Create file at .aidocs/features/{backlog|todo}/{NN}-{name}/spec.md
Fill metadata: Version, Status (Draft), Author, Date, Project
Add Reference Documents table linking to constitution.md
Write 2-3 sentences explaining feature purpose
Describe what users can do with this feature
Link to related features if applicable
Define WHO — which persona(s) affected
State PROBLEM — what they cannot do currently
Explain IMPACT — business or user consequences
Outline SOLUTION — high-level approach (no implementation details)
Define SUCCESS METRIC — measurable outcome
Create minimum 2 distinct personas
For each: Role, Goal, Pain Points, Context (when/where they use product)
Use specific archetype names (not "User A")
Create US-001, US-002, etc. with meaningful titles
Format: "As a [persona], I want [action], so that [benefit]"
Assign MoSCoW priority (Must/Should/Could/Won't)
Link to acceptance criteria (AC-001, etc.)
Create FR-001, FR-002, etc. table with ID, Requirement, Traces To, Priority
Write each requirement as "System SHALL [specific action]"
Ensure one interpretation only — no ambiguous language
Link each FR to the user story it traces to
Assign MoSCoW priority to every FR
Create NFR-001, NFR-002, etc. table with ID, Category, Requirement, Target, Priority
Cover: Performance, Security, Scalability, Availability (as applicable)
Every NFR must have a specific numeric target
Assign MoSCoW priority to every NFR
Create AC-001, AC-002, etc. with scenario titles
Use Given-When-Then format with specific values
Cover happy path (successful scenario)
Cover error cases (validation failures)
Cover boundary conditions (limits, edge cases)
Verify each criterion is testable with pass/fail
List specific features that were discussed and explicitly rejected
For each: state reason (Phase 2, Never, To be decided) and timeline
Minimum 3 out-of-scope items to demonstrate deliberate scoping
List all assumptions about user behavior
List all assumptions about system state
List technical constraints (limits, integrations)
List business constraints
Identify internal dependencies (other features needed first)
Identify external dependencies (APIs, services)
List related features with relationship type (depends on, blocks, related)
Problem statement is SMART and clear
All personas defined with Role, Goal, Pain Points, Context
All user stories have "so that" benefit clauses
All FRs have traceability to US-X
All NFRs have specific numeric targets
All ACs use Given-When-Then format with specific values
Out of Scope names specific rejected features (not generic)
No implementation details in spec
Status advanced to Review before handing off

text.reference: Complete spec.md template with all required sections
Script to verify FR-to-US and FR-to-AC traceability
